data.body:
G3 (child coverage) — parent 5fd2 × child 6c5c (Docs): CLEAN, no findings.

4-bucket coverage audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria. This child is the named owner of the parent's **Docs** AC ("Docs: how the gate works, the config surface, fail-open behavior, the cert semantics, the CI-check pattern, an HONEST end-to-end time-to-first-work (not just the 50ms fast claim-check), and the Pass-4 affirmative coach (matching child `6c5c`'s deliverables)"). The parent AC text anchors 1:1 to this child by id, so the mapping is explicit.

Sub-topic-by-sub-topic (parent Docs AC → child 6c5c):
- how the gate works → FULLY (child AC1 "behavior" + What "How the gate works (three-pass)").
- config surface → FULLY (child AC1 "config (incl. the disable preference)").
- fail-open behavior → FULLY (child AC1 "fail-open").
- cert semantics → FULLY (child AC1 "cert semantics").
- CI-check pattern → FULLY (child AC1 "CI pattern").
- honest end-to-end time-to-first-work (not just the 50ms claim check) → FULLY (child AC4, verbatim).
- Pass-4 affirmative coach (move registry + delivery + advisory-only) → FULLY (child AC3).

No uncovered parent Docs sub-topic. No overclaim: every child AC is a docs deliverable inside the parent's Docs AC; none reaches into sibling-owned territory. The round-5 residual "make the CI rigor predicate canonical in-repo" is an implementation concern owned by the attestation/claim children (4bb7/092b), not a docs-child coverage gap — this child correctly documents the CI-check *pattern*.

Note (out of G3 scope, already remediated): the child AC's "kill-switch" wording vs the parent SC#4 "ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch" is a terminology/consistency point (G4), not a coverage gap — the disable-config topic IS covered. It was flagged in prior G6 reviews on this ticket and reconciled in the shipped doc (the disable is documented as `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim`, default off). No G3 action.

Empty findings list for G3 on this pairing.
